title: Use cryptographically secure random numbers
description: Pseudorandom number generators (PRNGs) that are not cryptographically secure can produce predictable values, making them unsuitable for security-sensitive operations like generating tokens, salts, or cryptographic keys.
recommendation: Use cryptographically secure random number generators for security-sensitive operations.

noncompliant.description: Using math/rand for security-sensitive operations produces predictable values that attackers can anticipate.

compliant.description: Using crypto/rand for security-sensitive operations ensures cryptographically secure randomness that cannot be predicted by attackers.

checks.message: Use cryptographically secure random number generators for security-sensitive operations.
